title: Params for iam.credentials_{disable,enable,rotate}
description: Canonical parameters for the three credential-lifecycle actions sharing a shape : disable, enable, and rotate. The subject identifies the credential itself (AccessKeyId, key URI, key resource path) ; principal_id identifies the principal that OWNS the credential. AWS IAM hard-requires this 2-tuple (UserName + AccessKeyId) ; Azure and GCP usually self-identify via the credential identifier alone but the orchestrator playbook MUST still know whose credential it is touching, so the canonical contract demands principal_id explicitly. Connectors that ignore principal_id (because their vendor encodes it in the credential identifier) MAY do so without breaking conformance.

principal_id.description: The principal owning the credential. AWS : the IAM UserName. Azure : the application object id or service principal object id. GCP : the service account email or full resource path. Required across vendors so audit chains are unambiguous about whose credential changed.
